phase1.explanation:
Methods are reusable blocks of code:

Declaration: static returnType name(parameters) { body }

Return types: void (returns nothing) or a data type

Parameters: variables passed into the method

Method overloading: same name, different parameters
• Different number of parameters
• Different types of parameters

Benefits of methods:
• Code reuse — write once, use many times
• Organization — break complex problems into smaller pieces
• Readability — meaningful method names

Pro tip: Make methods do ONE thing and do it well!

phase2.errorMessage:
MethodsExample.java:7: error: invalid method declaration; return type required
    static add(int a, int b) {
           ^
1 error
phase2.explanation:
Every method MUST have a return type! Even void must be explicitly declared.

Correct: static int add(int a, int b)
or: static void add(int a, int b)

Always include the return type in your method declaration.

phase3.prompt:
Write a program with these methods:
1. multiply(int a, int b) — returns a * b
2. multiply(int a, int b, int c) — returns a * b * c (overloaded)
3. printMessage(String msg) — prints "=== [msg] ==="

Call all three methods from main.
dialogueHints: The first multiply takes 2 parameters, the second takes 3 (overloading)
printMessage should be void — it just prints
Call all methods in main with test values
phase3.openingDialogue: Time to create your own reusable blocks — methods! 🛠️
